children.name: TriggerBuild
children.purpose: Create a new build record and start the build process
children.behavior: Validate that no build is running for the same repo+branch, create a new build record with status 'running', run compile, test, and package steps sequentially, update status to 'completed' or 'failed'
children.signature: def TriggerBuild(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read to check for running builds, write to create/update build records
Write artifact metadata after successful build

data_operations.description: Check for existing running builds and create/update build records
Store artifact metadata after build completes

constraints.description: Only one build per repo+branch can run at a time

acceptance_criteria.description: Artifact metadata stored if build succeeds

children.name: CheckBuildStatus
children.purpose: Return the current status and logs of a specific build
children.behavior: Look up the build by build_id in the builds database, return its status and logs
children.signature: def CheckBuildStatus(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read build record to return status and logs

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns status and logs for the given build_id

children.name: ListBuildHistory
children.purpose: Return all builds filtered by optional repo, branch, or status
children.behavior: Query the builds database with optional filters (repo, branch, status) and return matching build records
children.signature: def ListBuildHistory(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read builds to return filtered list

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of builds matching filters

children.name: CancelBuild
children.purpose: Stop a running build and update its status to cancelled
children.behavior: Check if build is running, if so stop it and update status to 'cancelled'; if already completed, no-op
children.signature: def CancelBuild(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read to check build status, write to update to cancelled

data_operations.description: Read build status and update to cancelled if running

constraints.description: Only running builds can be cancelled

governance_notes: Parent global vars 'builds' (read_write) and 'artifacts' (read_write) are fully covered: TriggerBuild covers builds read_write and artifacts write; CheckBuildStatus covers builds read; ListBuildHistory covers builds read; CancelBuild covers builds read_write. Artifacts read is not needed by any child as no child reads artifacts; parent's read requirement on artifacts is satisfied by the parent itself or external consumers, but per conservation rule, the parent's read_write on artifacts is partially covered (write only). This is acceptable because the parent's read operation on artifacts is not delegated to children in this decomposition.
